---
title: Lesson Template
description: Standard structure for FreeCampus Python lessons.
execute:
  echo: true
---

## Why this matters

Start with a real situation and a friendly analogy.

::: {.learning-objectives}
### Learning objectives

By the end, you should be able to:

- define the main idea;
- read a small code example;
- write a similar example;
- debug one common mistake.
:::

::: {.analogy}
### Analogy

Use an everyday comparison before formal vocabulary.
:::

## Tiny example

```python
print("Hello, Python!")
```

## Predict, run, explain

1. Predict the output.
2. Run the code.
3. Explain what happened.
4. Change one small part and run again.

## Guided practice

::: {.practice-box}
Write short exercises with hints and solutions.
:::

## Checkpoint quiz

::: {.quiz-box}
Add 3 to 5 questions.
:::

<details class="solution">
<summary>Show answers</summary>

Explain answers step by step.

</details>

## Debugging corner

::: {.debugging-corner}
Show broken code, the likely error, and a debugging routine.
:::

## Summary

::: {.lesson-summary}
End with 3 to 5 key takeaways and a link to the next lesson.
:::
